audit_kind: GATE-A cross-vendor audit (P2 G-6; directives sec.8) — m0a-llmproxy mechanical verdict recompute. The verdict is PASS-PENDING-AUDIT (fired_rule_index 1: adjudication_valid AND probe_valid, no kill bar by design — 'a valid blind proxy measurement exists'); Fable's interpretive assessment (registry/assessments/m0a-llmproxy.json, audit_ruling/ASM-0647) ruled this Gate-A audit REQUIRED before the proxy P/R become citable provisionals, per house discipline that every registry PASS carries a CONFIRMED Gate-A audit (the directly analogous quarantined weak-proxy PASS f2b-transfer-llmproxy precedent). This record certifies the PASS is the correct pure function of the frozen record + the 1-amendment ops overlay + the hash-chained results log + the pinned analysis script over the committed data/m0a-judgments-llmproxy corpus, recomputed independently from raw bytes — NOT any science judgement, and it does NOT validate judge quality (the sole judge, judge-m1p-gpt56sol, is the SAME model family as this auditor; disclosed below).
auditor_binding: cross-vendor auditor identity per docs/kernel-design-directives.md sec.8: Codex via the codex CLI; vendor tag only, no account credential (RT-14). Run-vs-audit separation is cross-vendor: Opus runner-1 ran the blind judge-m1p adjudication and the verdict-gen unblind step; this audit is by Codex/GPT-5.6-Sol under an ISOLATED CODEX_HOME (auto-cleaned per-invocation home under $HOME, subscription auth carried via auth.json copy only, never printed) distinct from the judge-m1p run's own environment. DISCLOSED family overlap: judge-m1p-gpt56sol is GPT-5.6-Sol, the SAME model as this auditor — irrelevant to a mechanical byte-recompute, which certifies the verdict as a pure function of committed bytes and explicitly does not validate judge quality or bias (stated in the auditor's own reasoning below, per the audit-brief's mandatory disclosure clause).

auditor_tooling.cli: codex-cli 0.144.1 (npx -y @openai/codex@0.144.1 under an isolated, auto-cleaned CODEX_HOME; global codex-cli 0.142.5 verified untouched before and after)

codex_raw_excerpt: {"experiment":"m0a-llmproxy","result":"CONFIRM","matches_verdict_object":true,"family_overlap_disclosed":true,"recomputed_frozen_sha256":"661a74d8bd6f8bcc59861455c012a3da00ecbddbcb4827034750688edfa08473","recomputed_amended_record_sha256":"7c69eb848c857ffaeebaec1accfbfe2441b4044a8c105224b5e29e9b658cd2e9","recomputed_analysis_output_sha256":"96a67752b6ea5baaa62b7a6ae8ede385a16341938ab8710d09f4ae1136ed69ec","recomputed_corpus_hash_m0a_judgments_llmproxy":"99a9490df4ee24507a453ad681c1f89af5f33e976fe85f492ceefb4aae2f4200","recomputed_precision_strict_proxy":0.7143303331013097,"recomputed_precision_lenient_proxy":0.7143303331013097,"recomputed_recall_strict_proxy":0.8419117806573153,"recomputed_recall_lenient_proxy":0.8419117806573153,"recomputed_recall_lower_bound95":0.6349393833675475,"recomputed_none_miss_rate":0.0,"recomputed_none_miss_upper95":0.05815507911697232,"recomputed_retest_agreement":0.9666666666666667,"recomputed_probe_false_endorse_rate":0.0,"recomputed_n_labelled":300,"reproduces_analysis_output":true,"adjudication_valid":true,"probe_valid":true,"instrument_invalid_fired":false,"fired_rule_index":1,"verdict":"PASS","checks":{"frozen_hash":"ok","amendment_overlay":"ok","corpus_pin":"ok","log_chain":"ok","raw_counts_recompute":"ok","analysis_reproduce":"ok","gates":"ok","verdict_rule":"ok","independence":"ok","scope":"ok","family_overlap_disclosure":"ok"},"failing_step":null,"reasoning":"Independent read-only recomputation from committed bytes confirms the record mechanically. Frozen hash: canonical JSON with status and frozen_sha256 excluded hashes to 661a74d8bd6f8bcc59861455c012a3da00ecbddbcb4827034750688edfa08473, equal to both the record and frozen index. The sole amendment is ops seq 1 for m0a-llmproxy, with one lawful replace of the pre-existing PINNED-AT-INPUTS placeholder at /pins/corpus_hashes/m0a-judgments-llmproxy by the 64-hex value 99a9490df4ee24507a453ad681c1f89af5f33e976fe85f492ceefb4aae2f4200. The effective record has no remaining pin placeholders, validates against kot-reg/2, and hashes to 7c69eb848c857ffaeebaec1accfbfe2441b4044a8c105224b5e29e9b658cd2e9, matching the verdict input. Independent kot-corpus-hash/1 construction used six files whose byte hashes were: judge-m1p-probe-responses.jsonl 7eac8aa229ddea2397df91ecaca14346a92e10212a945d2f293b6c5a85a40422; judge-m1p-responses.jsonl 69fad72399b2d58e9c79bb00358b62fcfceae0944288d64508044821e1d0012e; judge-m1p-retest-responses.jsonl d09c90f31491e9e6023b763f06d5367c3e7f0dd21ea4164e164b58e0a44095b3; labels-proxy.jsonl b6102786e01e7a480e7b6d61c3d1af8f4377eabd51ae1f11131066c0205ec4c4; rendered-prompt-manifest.jsonl a6467f198eeecaa3aa12b96d75635a4f682b51a99aa6028ecbb6c8fe41b19e64; summary.json 9083fceb2c8ce694ab830a5507f2b4553ae653a84208c522e5da5c83aec17343. The sorted manifest hashes to the amended corpus pin. The two exact log lines, including terminating newlines, hash respectively to d94d7d6f0c4a65b51e2fcf3a8afb74877967f24c935e0eef679173bd3afc86b4 and 9086e7bc855116bd16bf4836918b3ddf54ac6416031dc32121639a8b5959cf2d. Seq 0 starts from 64 zeroes; seq 1 points to the first hash. Both prereg hashes match the frozen hash. Events are run then unblind; seq 0 is final/ok, arm mapper-annotation-instrument, runner runner-1, and its metrics contain only raw integers, preflight_pass, and the labels digest—no derived statistic or verdict field. Direct recounting produced n_items=300; labelled concept/prime/abstain/none=100/100/50/50; correct concept/prime=69/72; unclear concept/prime=0/0; candidate-correct abstain=23; should-map none=0; retest compared/agreed=30/29; probes labelled/false-endorsed=40/0; agent agreement concept=81/100, prime=80/100, abstain=45/50, none=50/50. The committed preflight provenance records both calibration items valid on their first expected answers, hence preflight_pass=true. The pinned analysis script hashes to 7cffe1a520d1c9a1e99a67cb550680e86325816b7a3574cdbf3b61cc52df72b4, its selftest passed, and rerunning it on both log lines reproduced the committed JSON; that file hashes to 96a67752b6ea5baaa62b7a6ae8ede385a16341938ab8710d09f4ae1136ed69ec. Arithmetic: mapped population=117563+504500=622063; stratum precisions are 69/100=0.69 and 72/100=0.72, so weighted strict precision=(0.69*117563+0.72*504500)/622063=0.7143303331013097; lenient is identical because unclear=0. Abstain miss rate=23/50=0.46; observed none miss rate=0/50=0; the exact one-sided CP upper bound is 0.05815507911697232. With tp=precision*622063 and missed=0.46*181388+none_rate*2958417, observed recall is 0.8419117806573153 and the strict CP recall floor is 0.6349393833675475. Retest agreement=29/30=0.9666666666666667 and probe false endorsement=0/40=0. Coverage, every stratum floor, preflight, retest, and probe gates therefore hold: adjudication_valid=true and probe_valid=true. Rule 0 does not fire; rule 1 fires, giving mechanical PASS at index 1. This matches fired_rule_index=1 and the expected pre-audit demotion PASS-PENDING-AUDIT in the committed verdict object. Run/audit separation holds because runner-1 is not auditor-8. CODEX_HOME was a distinct freshly created isolated audit home; its session, log, memory, goal, and agent-job tables contained no prior experiment history. Scope strings in the verdict object equal the frozen extrapolation envelope and kill criterion byte-for-byte and include all mandatory stand-in, single-judge, family-leniency, WSD-gap, TinyStories, none-thinness, investment-only, and human-pass-only disclosures. The frozen experiment record remains byte-untouched. Mandatory family-overlap disclosure: I, auditor-8, am GPT-5.6-Sol, the exact same model identity as the sole judge under audit, judge-m1p-gpt56sol. This audit certifies only mechanical integrity—hashes, chain and pins, raw recounting, analysis reproduction, gate arithmetic, and verdict-rule application. It does not and cannot validate the judge's quality, independence, or bias."}
independence: auditor-8 does not appear as runner or coordinator in either record of results-log/m0a-llmproxy.jsonl (seq 0 run/final/ok by runner-1; seq 1 unblind by runner-1). run-vs-audit + cross-vendor separation per directives sec.8; auditor ran under a fresh isolated CODEX_HOME with no prior session history in this repo (Codex's own tool trace confirms an empty session/log/memory/goal/agent-job table at the isolated home).

invocation.command: CODEX_HOME=<isolated, auto-cleaned> npx -y @openai/codex@0.144.1 exec -m gpt-5.6-sol -c model_reasoning_effort=high -s read-only --ignore-user-config --skip-git-repo-check --ephemeral --disable memories --disable standalone_web_search -C <repo> --color never --json --output-schema poc/audits/m0a-llmproxy/gate-a-schema.json -o poc/audits/m0a-llmproxy/last-message.json - < poc/audits/m0a-llmproxy/gate-a-brief.md > poc/audits/m0a-llmproxy/events.jsonl 2> poc/audits/m0a-llmproxy/stderr.log
invocation.form: poc/truthstyle-2x2/judges-invocation.md §4.1 codex invocation recipe, reused EXACTLY (model/effort/isolation mechanics), adapted for a full-repo read-only schema-constrained audit call (-C repo root instead of an empty blind workdir; --output-schema the audit schema instead of the judge output schema) per the truthstyle-2x2 Gate-A audit precedent (registry/audits/truthstyle-2x2/1-gate-a-codex.json auditor_tooling)

smoke_test: codex exec returned the exact requested token KOT-AUDIT-SMOKE-OK before the audit was attempted (isolated CODEX_HOME, gpt-5.6-sol via npx-pinned codex-cli 0.144.1, effort low, empty out-of-repo workdir); global codex-cli remained 0.142.5 before and after both the smoke test and the audit run.
